issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The button defined by `SELECTORS.navToggle` (class `.nav-toggle`) toggles the off‑canvas navigation drawer but does not have an `aria-controls` attribute because the drawer element lacks an `id`. Without this relationship, screen‑reader users cannot discover which region the button controls.
issuesFound.fix: Give the drawer element a unique `id` (e.g. `id="nav-drawer"`) and add `aria-controls="nav-drawer"` to the toggle button.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates not announced via live region (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: In `productFilter._updateCountBadge` the visible count of matching products is changed, but the element has no `aria-live` or other announcement mechanism. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the result set changed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `aria-live="polite"` (and optionally `aria-atomic="true"`) to the count badge element, or update a dedicated live‑region with the new count after each filter operation.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard progress bar changes not announced via live region (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: `multiStepWizard._updateProgress` updates the visual progress bar (`aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuetext`) but does not provide an `aria-live` announcement. Screen‑reader users are not informed when the wizard advances to a new step.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the progress bar, set the text of an element with `role="status"` (or `aria-live="polite"`) to something like “Step 2 of 5”.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: In `newsletterForm._onSubmit` the email field is validated with `validateField`, but when the validation fails the code only shows a status message; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the `<input type="email">`. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: When validation fails, call `setAttribute('aria-invalid','true')` on the email input (and remove it on success).

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: `discountCodeInput._onApply` validates the entered code and displays a status message, but does not set `aria-invalid` on the `<input>` when the code is invalid. Users of screen readers cannot perceive the error.
issuesFound.fix: If validation fails, add `setAttribute('aria-invalid','true')` to the discount‑code input and remove it when the code becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay hidden via `hidden` attribute but not `aria‑hidden` (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the overlay element (`_overlay`) is hidden with `setAttribute('hidden','')` but never receives `aria-hidden="true"`. Because the element remains in the accessibility tree, screen‑reader focus can be trapped on an invisible overlay.
issuesFound.fix: When hiding the overlay, also set `aria-hidden="true"`; when showing it, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state element shown/hidden without ARIA state (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: The empty‑state message (`_emptyState`) is toggled with `hidden`/`removeAttribute('hidden')` but lacks `aria-hidden`. Assistive technologies may still announce the element when it is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Mirror the visibility changes with `setAttribute('aria-hidden','true')` when hidden and remove the attribute when shown.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels hidden with CSS class only, missing aria‑hidden (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.close`, the panel is animated with `animateHeight` and then `setAttribute('hidden','')`, but the `aria-hidden` attribute is not updated until after the animation completes, leaving a brief period where the hidden panel is still exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Immediately set `aria-hidden="true"` when starting the hide animation, and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slides lack `aria-live` for slide changes (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: `testimonialsCarousel._announce` uses `liveRegion.announce` to speak the slide number, but the slide elements themselves do not have `role="region"` with `aria-live`. If the live region is removed or fails, users receive no update.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden live‑region inside each slide (or a single region) that receives the slide label on change, ensuring the announcement is always tied to the DOM change.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher buttons do not expose current state via aria‑pressed (JS‑B)
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle buttons (`[data-theme-toggle]`) change the site theme but only use `aria-pressed` on the button that was clicked; the other buttons retain `aria-pressed="false"` only on init, not dynamically when the theme changes via other means (e.g., system preference). This can leave the ARIA state out of sync with the actual theme.
issuesFound.fix: In `themeSwitcher.set`, after updating the theme, iterate all `[data-theme-toggle]` buttons and set `aria-pressed` to `"true"` for the button matching the current preference and `"false"` for the others.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function hides the drawer by removing CSS classes and scrolling lock, but it never adds `aria‑hidden="true"` to the drawer element. When `aria‑hidden` is not set, the hidden drawer remains in the accessibility tree, causing screen‑reader users to encounter off‑screen navigation links.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
  function close(announceToSR) {
    …
    _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');   // add this line
    …
  }
  ```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not remove `aria‑hidden` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function makes the drawer visible by adding CSS classes, but it never removes `aria‑hidden` or sets it to `false`. The drawer stays announced as hidden to assistive technologies even though it is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
  function open(announceToSR) {
    …
    _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');      // add this line
    …
  }
  ```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are not cleared of `aria‑hidden` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()`, the panel is shown by removing the `hidden` attribute, but the code never removes `aria‑hidden`. Consequently, the panel remains hidden from AT despite being visible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are not given `aria‑hidden="true"` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()`, the panel is hidden by adding the `hidden` attribute, but `aria‑hidden` is never set to `true`. The panel therefore stays in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
  function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) {
    …
    inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');   // add this line
    …
  }
  ```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels do not clear `aria‑hidden` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function removes the `hidden` attribute from the answer panel but does not remove `aria‑hidden`. The panel stays hidden from screen readers even though it is displayed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels do not set `aria‑hidden="true"` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.close()` function adds the `hidden` attribute to the answer panel but never adds `aria‑hidden="true"`. The panel remains in the accessibility tree while hidden.
issuesFound.fix:

```javascript
    …

    …
  }
  ```

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: In `suggestionListRenderer.hide()`, the listbox is hidden by adding the `hidden` attribute, but `aria‑hidden` is not set to `true`. The list remains perceivable to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav overlay (`[data-nav-overlay]`) missing `aria-hidden` when hidden

issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function adds the “open” class and removes hidden from the panel, but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger may be missing aria‑controls link to its panel
issuesFound.explanation: register sets aria-controls only if the panel already has an id. If the panel lacks an id, the trigger has no aria‑controls, breaking the relationship between button and menu.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each panel receives a unique id (e.g., panel.id = `dropdown-panel-${shortId()}`) and always set aria-controls on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the code animates the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening the item.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion question buttons lack an initial aria‑expanded="false" attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The parser does not set aria‑expanded on the trigger when the item is collapsed by default, leaving the attribute undefined on page load.
issuesFound.fix: After parsing, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); for all collapsed items.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden via CSS class without aria‑hidden or hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing the “is‑open” class, but no aria‑hidden or hidden attribute is applied, so the drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In close, add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and _drawer.setAttribute('hidden',''); when the drawer is closed.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑expanded or aria‑pressed to convey selection state
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (data‑period) change visual state but never expose their selected state via aria‑expanded or aria‑pressed, making it impossible for screen‑reader users to know which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In _setPeriod or _syncToggleState, set setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); or aria-expanded accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑controls to the price display region
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons control the price values shown in the plan cards but do not have aria‑controls pointing to the region that updates, violating the requirement for a programmatic relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Assign each button aria-controls to the id of the shared price container (e.g., setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'price-display')).

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle buttons use aria‑pressed but the script only updates the attribute on click, not on initial load
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, the buttons do not have aria‑pressed set to reflect the persisted theme, so the initial state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After reading the stored theme, call setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle')===currentTheme)); for each button.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not set aria‑describedby or aria‑label to describe its purpose
issuesFound.explanation: The drop zone element receives no accessible name or description, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of its function.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(zoneEl, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-label', 'Upload files – click or drag files here'); or associate a visible label via aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list items lack role="option" and aria‑selected updates are not reflected in the live region when navigating with arrow keys
issuesFound.explanation: While each <li> has role="option", the live region does not announce the currently highlighted suggestion when the user navigates with the keyboard, causing loss of context.
issuesFound.fix: After setActiveIndex, call liveRegion.announce(`Suggestion ${index+1} of ${count}: ${item.label}`); or set aria‑activedescendant on the input to the active <li> id.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button click handler does not update aria‑expanded (dropdown menus)
issuesFound.explanation: `_onTriggerClick()` toggles a dropdown’s open state via class changes but never updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger button, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After opening set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; after closing set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the question button, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a FAQ item updates the UI but provides no live‑region announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the newly revealed answer.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened?.replace('{title}', item.question) ?? 'Answer expanded');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` marks fields as invalid visually but only adds `aria-describedby`; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on valid input). This violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid` call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is rendered without an initial `aria-expanded` value, so the accessibility tree does not reflect the closed state on load.
issuesFound.fix: In the markup or during initialization, set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger missing aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons are created without an initial `aria-expanded` attribute, so their collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: When parsing each FAQ item, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` if the item is initially closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` hides the drawer via CSS classes but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` (or removes it on open). The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: When closing, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, `removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden with `hidden` attribute but no ARIA state announced on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: Opening/closing a dropdown updates `hidden` but does not provide a live‑region announcement, so screen‑reader users are not informed of the menu’s visibility change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening/closing, use `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or similar.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile nav drawer changes the UI but does not emit any live‑region message, leaving users of assistive technology unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After `open()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen ?? 'Navigation menu opened');` and after `close()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed ?? 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Toggling a dropdown updates visibility but provides no live‑region feedback, so screen‑reader users cannot tell when the submenu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened ?? 'Submenu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed ?? 'Submenu closed');`.

  ```tsx

    value={name.state.value}
    onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)}
    onBlur={name.onBlur}
    invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
    aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? "contact-name-error" : undefined}

  ```

issuesFound.explanation: The link marked as current via CSS class does not have aria‑current="page", so screen readers cannot identify the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `<a href={item.href}` `className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}` `aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation <nav> missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation <nav> element is not labelled, and because there is more than one <nav> on the page (desktop and mobile), AT cannot differentiate them.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation <nav> missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria‑labelledby reference

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list missing aria‑hidden

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop missing aria‑hidden

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay missing aria‑hidden (decorative)
